pqs.question: Has the State promulgated specific operating regulations to transpose the provisions of Annex 6 (Parts I, II and III)?
criteria: Confirm the title, date of promulgation and last amendment of all regulations related to aircraft operations.
Verify that the content of the regulations is consistent, sufficient (addressing all audit areas as needed) and properly organized.
Confirm the transposition of the aircraft operations-related provisions of Annex 6, Parts I, II and III.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented procedures for amending its enabling aircraft operations regulations, taking into consideration ICAO provisions and their amendments?
pqs.note: The establishment of amendment procedures is addressed in LEG PQ 1.009. Procedures for amending regulations on the transport of dangerous goods by air are addressed in OPS PQ 4.333.
criteria: Review documented evidence of effective implementation of procedures for amending aircraft operations regulations.
Verify that regulations are amended in a timely manner whenever amendments to the related Annex are received.
Verify the action taken by the State after receipt of the last amendments to Annex 6, Parts I, II and III.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented procedures for identifying and notifying differences, if any, to ICAO?
pqs.note: The establishment of these procedures is addressed in LEG PQ 1.025.
criteria: Review documented evidence of the effective implementation.

Confirm that the differences between the SARPs and the State legislation and practices are identified and notified to ICAO using the Compliance Checklist/Electronic Filing of Differences (CC/EFOD) system.
Confirm that significant differences between SARPs/PANS/SUPPS and the State regulations and practices are identified and published in the AIP.

pqs.question: Does the State have provisions in its legislation or regulations making it mandatory for any civil aircraft under its registry or operated by its air operators to comply with interception orders from other States?
criteria: Review legislation/regulations to ensure that compliance with interception orders from other States is mandatory for civil aircraft under the State's registry or operated by its air operators.

pqs.question: Has the State established requirements regarding documents to be carried on board an aircraft in compliance with Article 29 of the Chicago Convention and Annex 6, Parts I, II, and III, as applicable?
pqs.note: The requirement on maintaining a journey log book is addressed in OPS PQ 4.018.
pqs.lead: Verify that the required documents to be carried on board include:
criteria: Certificate of registration.
Certificate of airworthiness.
Crew members’ licences.
Journey log book.
Aircraft radio station licence, if it has a radio apparatus.
Passenger manifest, if it carries passengers.
Cargo manifest and detailed declarations of the cargo, if it carries cargo.
Certified true copy of the AOC.
Operations specifications relevant to the aircraft type, issued in conjunction with the AOC.
Document attesting noise certification.

pqs.question: Does the State require that a journey log book be maintained in respect of every aircraft engaged in international navigation?
criteria: Review legislation/regulations requiring that a journey log book be maintained for every aircraft engaged in international navigation.
Review examples of journey log books.

pqs.question: Has the State established an organizational structure for aircraft operations, such as a directorate/department/division/section/office for the certification and surveillance of commercial air transport operations, and general aviation operations, and does it include the oversight of the transport of dangerous goods by air?
pqs.lead: Verify that an organizational structure has been:
criteria: Established.
Documented.
Approved.
Confirm that the organizational structure is depicited on charts or equivalent documents.
Confirm that the structural layout details functions related to dangerous goods oversight.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented documented procedures for coordination with other relevant areas, including personnel licensing, airworthiness, legal, financial etc., on the air operator certification processes and surveillance activities?
criteria: Verify that the organizational structure identifies the authority responsible for the issuance of licences, certificates, approval, authorizations and surveillance.
Verify that the operations inspectors manual contains processes and procedures for coordination on all certification and surveillance activities involving operations.
Verify effective implementation with airworthiness.
Verify effective implementation with personnel licensing.
Verify effective implementation with other entities, as applicable.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization have sufficient human resources to carry out its functions and mandate related to its safety oversight responsibilities?
pqs.lead: Review the resources that are required for safety oversight-related tasks, including:
criteria: Review the methodology established for determining staffing needs.
Review the ability to attract new inspectors as well as existing vacancies and level of turnover in the past years.
Review capability to execute reviewing and revising regulations.
Review capability to execute training of technical staff.
Review capability to execute developing guidance material.
Review capability to execute issuing approvals.
Review capability to execute conducting surveillance.
Review capability to execute resolving identified safety concerns.
Consider the resources that may be required for the area of Dangerous Goods.

pqs.question: Are all the functions and responsibilities of the flight operations inspection organization clearly defined?
pqs.lead: Review the document detailing functions and responsibilities for the inclusion of:
criteria: Certification process of air operators.
Issuance of AOC and operations specifications.
Issuance of specific approvals (EDTO, PBN authorization required (AR), RVSM, AWOP, EFB, and transport of dangerous goods by air).
Development and/or revision of specific operating regulations.
Certification of general aviation operations.
Supervision of commercial air operators and legal action/information regarding deficiencies.

pqs.question: Have job descriptions been developed for technical staff and key management personnel of the flight operations inspection organization and dangerous goods authority?
pqs.lead: Verify that job descriptions have been developed for:
criteria: Technical staff.
Management personnel.
Dangerous goods personnel.

pqs.question: Has the flight operations inspection organization established appropriate minimum qualifications and experience requirements for operations and dangerous goods inspectors?
pqs.lead: Verify that minimum qualifications and experience requirements include:
criteria: Specified number of years in air transport operations.
To be at least as qualified as the personnel to be inspected or supervised.
Experience compatible with the activities that they are authorized to certify or supervise.

pqs.question: Have operations inspectors been issued credentials to facilitate their access to facilities, aircraft, and certification and licensing documentation for the purpose of inspections and enforcement?
pqs.lead: Review credentials to ensure that they include the following:
criteria: Reference to empowering legislation.
Methods established to control currency of credentials.
Inspector’s photo.

pqs.question: If the flight operations inspection organization delegates its duties to other entities, have requirements for competency, qualifications, and experience been established for each delegated authority?
pqs.note: Includes CAA divisions, other State bodies, Contracting States, regional organizations, private agencies or individuals.
criteria: Competency requirements have been established for each delegated authority.
Appropriate minimum qualifications and experience requirements have been established for each delegated authority.

pqs.question: If the flight operations inspection organization delegates its duties to other entities, are the delegated tasks clearly defined?
criteria: There is documentation that clearly defines delegated tasks (e.g. letter of agreement, letter of designation of examiners, etc.).
Verify legal basis of each delegation.

pqs.question: If the flight operations inspection organization delegates any responsibilities to designated inspectors/examiners of an AOC holder, has it implemented a delegation procedure?
criteria: Selection criteria.
Examinations or tests.
Application process.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: If the flight operations inspection organization delegates responsibilities to designated inspectors/examiners of an AOC holder, has it implemented procedures for renewal of approval?
criteria: The approval specifies the duration of the designation.
Conditions for renewal are established and specified (e.g. minimum activity, training, etc.).
Review evidence to verify effective implementation of the renewal procedures.

pqs.question: If the flight operations inspection organization delegates responsibilities to designated inspectors/examiners of an AOC holder, has it implemented a surveillance system?
pqs.note: Surveillance activities include scheduled, unscheduled and unannounced activities.
criteria: A surveillance system has been established and is being implemented.
The surveillance activities undertaken conform to the established system.
The system includes on-the-job oversight activities of designated examiners.

pqs.question: If the flight operations inspection organization delegates responsibilities to designated inspectors/examiners of an AOC holder, has it implemented a system to resolve deficiencies and issues related to the conduct of delegated tasks?
pqs.note: If no surveillance is performed (see OPS 4.047), then this question cannot be satisfactory.
criteria: A system has been established and implemented to resolve deficiencies and issues related to the conduct of delegated tasks.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: Has the flight operations inspection organization established a formal training programme for operations inspectors and dangerous goods inspectors?
pqs.lead: Verify that each training programme details:
criteria: Initial training.
OJT.
Recurrent training.
Specialized training requirements.
Minimum duration for each type of training.
Confirm that specialized training is provided to ground operations, flight operations, cabin safety, and dangerous goods inspectors.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization establish and implement a periodic training plan detailing and prioritizing the type of training that will be provided to operations and dangerous goods inspectors during the established period?
criteria: Review most recent training plan for details of training type, priorities, and specified timeframe.
Verify that the training plan outlines the timetable for implementation for each staff member.
Check training files for course certificates.
Verify completed training (initial, recurrent, specialized, OJT) is sufficient to maintain required competence.
Verify training is implemented appropriately for operations and dangerous goods inspectors.
Verify there is a requirement for OJT, and that it is provided by an experienced, senior inspector.

pqs.question: Do the flight operations inspection organization and the dangerous goods entities have a system for maintaining training records of operations and dangerous goods inspectors?
criteria: Review requirements/instructions for the establishment and maintenance of training records.
Review the system in place.
Sample training records.

pqs.question: Do operations and dangerous goods inspectors have access to up-to-date operational documentation and information to support their certification/approval and surveillance activities?
pqs.note: Documents may be available on the Internet, in paper copies, CDs, etc.
pqs.lead: Verify accessibility of up-to-date documentation, such as:
criteria: Operations manual.
Aircraft flight manual.
Master Minimum Equipment List (MMEL).
Loading manual.
Weight and balance manual.
Dangerous goods manuals/programme.
Check document availability and the system used to keep them up to date.

pqs.question: Are ICAO documents and other technical publications readily available to the technical and administrative staff of the flight operations inspection organization?
pqs.note: Check for field/regional offices as well as Headquarters.
pqs.lead: Verify the availability of up-to-date copies of:
criteria: State laws and regulations.
Orders and instructions.
Current copy of Annexes 6 (Parts I, II and III), 18 and the Technical Instructions for the Safe Transport of Dangerous Goods by Air (Doc 9284).
Copies of ICAO guidance material (i.e. Doc 8335, Doc 10153, etc.).

pqs.question: Is there a technical library available for the staff of the flight operations inspection organization or another method to ensure receipt, control and distribution of the necessary technical documentation?
criteria: Evaluate document control system.
Evaluate method to determine currency of documents.

pqs.question: Are operations inspectors provided with comprehensive procedures and checklists to assist them in carrying out their functions and responsibilities effectively?
pqs.note: Procedures may be compiled into an inspector’s handbook.
pqs.lead: Verify that instructions and guidance cover:
criteria: Operations organization.
Operations inspectors’ duties, credentials and authority.
Certification process.
Issuance of operations specifications.
Guidelines on assessment of application forms.
Guidelines on renewal, suspension and revocation of AOCs and various authorizations.
Surveillance policies and programme.
Quality assurance.
Follow up on discrepancy and enforcement action.
Safety management programme.
Guidelines on resolution of safety issues.
Verify that there is a mechanism to approve the procedures and to ensure their currency.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization provide sufficient information to a prospective air operator regarding the regulations and process for obtaining an AOC?
pqs.lead: Verify that the standard information includes:
criteria: AOC application workflow.
Amendment to an AOC.
Amendment to operations specifications.
Authorizations or limitations.
Specific approval to transport dangerous goods.

pqs.question: Is there a formal application form that a prospective air operator must complete and submit in order to obtain an AOC?
pqs.lead: Verify that application forms contain at least the following information:
criteria: Main base of operations and organizational structure.
Type of operation (passenger, cargo, etc.).
Specific approvals requested (AWOP, RVSM, PBN authorization required (AR), Dangerous Goods, EDTO, EFB, etc.).
Type of aircraft.
Arrangements for crew and ground personnel training.
Proposed routes and areas of operations, destination and alternate aerodromes.
Maintenance arrangements.
Financial data and a business plan.

pqs.question: As part of a preliminary assessment of technical fitness, is there a general review of the procedures detailed in manuals?
criteria: Verify that the certification process and procedures include an assessment of the applicants’ technical fitness.
Verify that the operations inspectors have guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. to use in assessing technical fitness.
Verify implementation by sampling records of first meetings and letters to applicants showing first review of documents submitted.

pqs.question: Is the organizational structure of an AOC applicant reviewed to ensure defined duties, authorities, and functional reporting lines?
criteria: Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, etc. have been established and implemented.
Review exchange of letters with the applicant to confirm structural reviews.

pqs.question: Has the State effectively implemented procedures to grant exemptions from the regulations for an AOC to air operators?
pqs.note: Exemptions include exceptions, deviations, variations and extensions. Legal basis under LEG 1.027 and 1.028.
criteria: Verify effective implementation of formal exemption procedures.
Verify that the impact on safety is thoroughly considered.
Verify notification to foreign States if an AOC holder does not comply with Annex 6 standards.
Sample exemptions granted.

pqs.question: Has the flight operations inspection organization implemented a documented process for the certification of air operators that includes technical evaluations?
criteria: Verify that operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. provide a system to keep track of every exchange with the air operators.
Verify the filing system of the certification process.
Sample files to confirm structured technical evaluations.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that an AOC applicant maintains the validity of its manuals at all times?
criteria: Review surveillance policy and programme of air operators to verify that compliance with validity of manuals is monitored.
Sample completed inspection records/checklists.
Confirm effective implementation by verifying air operators’ manuals during industry visits.

pqs.question: Has the flight operations inspection organization established procedures to review and approve the contents and structure of an operations manual?
pqs.lead: Verify that procedures ensure that the operations manual contains:
criteria: Procedures established for the approval or acceptance of an air operator’s operations manual.
Compliance statement regarding applicable laws, AOC conditions, and operations specifications.
A list and description of manual parts, contents, applicability, and utilization rules.
Instructions making compliance mandatory for all operational personnel.
An amendment registration sheet mapping validation dates, with clear markings for text/graphic revisions.
Verify that inspectors check for the 4 core components: General, Aircraft Operating Information, Routes/Aerodromes, and Training.

pqs.question: Has the flight operations inspection organization established procedures for approval of MELs and procedures for the use of configuration deviation lists (CDLs)?
criteria: Verify procedures established for approval of MEL.
Verify procedures for the use of CDL.
Verify coordination channels between operations and airworthiness aspects.

pqs.question: Has the State promulgated regulations for AOC applicants to ensure that the flight manual is updated by changes from the State of Registry?
criteria: Verify the establishment and implementation of relevant State regulations.
Verify applicable certification process controls.
Verify operations inspectors’ evaluation procedures.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that air operators include instructions for the preservation of flight recorder records in safe custody?
pqs.note: Applies to custody pending disposition after an accident or incident.
criteria: Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.
Sample air operators’ manuals to confirm implementation and compliance.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator outlines the responsibilities of operations personnel regarding flight conduct?
criteria: Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.
Sample air operators’ manuals to confirm implementation and compliance.

pqs.question: Has the State promulgated regulations for flight time, flight duty period, duty period and rest period limitations based on scientific principles?
pqs.lead: Verify that prescriptive limitation regulations are promulgated for:
criteria: Flight crew fatigue management codes.
Cabin crew fatigue management codes.
Review regulations to verify they provide clear opportunity for sleep recovery.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator has requirements to establish crew rest and duty limitations in accordance with State regulations?
criteria: Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.
Sample air operators’ manuals to confirm implementation and compliance.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator outlines standard operating procedures (SOPs) and checklists?
pqs.lead: Verify that manuals incorporate detailed SOPs for:
criteria: Each distinctive phase of flight.
Crew briefings for departure and approach profiles.
Developed checklists as an integral part of its SOPs.
Evidence that the operator has instructed and trained its flight and cabin crew on how to use the SOPs.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator outlines instructions on the clarification and acceptance of air traffic control (ATC) clearances?
pqs.note: Particularly where terrain clearance is involved.
criteria: Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.
Sample air operators’ manuals to confirm implementation and compliance.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator includes instructions on the avoidance of CFIT and policy on the use of GPWS?
criteria: Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.
Sample air operators’ manuals to confirm implementation and compliance.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator lays down instructions and training requirements on collision avoidance and ACAS use?
criteria: Verify that the approval procedures are in accordance with PANS-OPS and PANS-ATM.
Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.
Sample air operators’ manuals to confirm implementation and compliance for procedures and training.
Review training evidence during industry visits to confirm implementation.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that an air operator has established a system to provide its operations staff with aircraft operating information?
pqs.note: Operating limitations are operator-specific approvals from the State and may be more restrictive than Type Certification.
pqs.lead: Verify that aircraft operating information includes:
criteria: Organization, update and revision tracking system.
Certification limitations and operating limitations as indicated in the AOC and OPSPEC.
Normal, abnormal and emergency flight procedures, related checklists, crew coordination, and PBN instructions.
Instructions for aircraft loading, along with data for mass and balance calculations.
Aircraft systems descriptions, associated controls, and instructions for their use.
Emergency evacuation procedures, including type-specific steps, crew coordination, and assignment configurations.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that aircraft operating information includes approved MEL and CDL parameters?
criteria: Verify implementation of procedures to issue the initial approval and approval of subsequent amendments.
Verify implementation of documented coordination procedures between AIR and OPS.
Sample an MEL and CDL, including applicable specific operations authorized (e.g. EDTO, all weather operations, RVSM, RNP, etc.).
Verify that the same amendment of the MEL is actively used within the air operator during industry visits.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that an air operator has established a route guide to be used by its flight crew?
criteria: Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.
Sample route guides to ensure they contain information relating to communication facilities, navigation aids, aerodromes, instrument approaches, arrivals, and departures.

pqs.question: Has the State promulgated regulations for the air operator to establish and maintain training programmes, as part of the flight safety documents system, which include initial, recurrent, transition (conversion), re-qualification, upgrade, recency of experience, familiarization, differences and other specialized training, as applicable?
criteria: Verify regulations.
Ensure that regulations cover all types of training, including human factors.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that an air operator outlines, in its training programmes, not only details of the initial and recurrent flight crew training, but also transition (conversion), re-qualification, upgrade, recency of experience, familiarization, differences and other specialized training, as applicable?
criteria: Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.
Sample some air operators’ manuals to confirm implementation and compliance.
Review evidence during industry visit to confirm implementation.

pqs.question: Has the flight operations inspection organization established procedures to verify that an air operator has outlined details of the cabin crew’s safety duties and functions in the cabin crew training programme?
criteria: Verify inspectors’ procedures to ensure that review of the cabin crew training programme is part of the procedures.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator takes into account, reviews and implements all regulations related to the contents of the operations manual, as part of the flight safety documents system, before the AOC or any specific approval is granted?
pqs.note: A clear chronological certification process must be demonstrated.
criteria: Verify inspectors’ procedures for approval of operations manual.
Verify that the inspection phase of the certification process is performed after the review of the manuals.
Verify that the operations manual is reviewed before granting of the AOC or any specific approval.
Confirm implementation of the certification process and results of inspections before granting the AOC.
Sample a specific case.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that: a) the air operator has established and maintained a flight data analysis programme; b) the flight data analysis programme is non-punitive and contains safeguards to protect the source(s) of the data?
criteria: Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.
Sample some air operators’ manuals to confirm implementation and compliance.
Review evidence during industry visit to confirm implementation.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that: a) the air operator provides ground and flight training facilities, simulators and/or cockpit procedure training devices (e.g. fixed-based simulator [FBS], computer based training [CBT], etc.) and syllabus materials; and b) if a separate training organization is approved to provide air operators’ flight crew training, does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the training provided and flight documentation used correctly reflect the air operators’ flight safety documents system?
criteria: Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.

Sample some air operators’ manuals to confirm implementation and compliance of: a) air operator’s training programme; b) list of approved training facilities (including approval of flight simulation training devices); and c) training programme and records.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization have procedures to: a) verify that all applicable regulations are addressed in the flight crew training programmes; and b) thoroughly analyse and inspect these programmes before granting the AOC or any specific approval?
pqs.lead: Review operations inspectors’ approval procedures to ensure that these are sufficiently detailed to cover all training and that the following are addressed:
criteria: Training policies and directives.
Administrative support of air operator.
List of designated instructors and line check examiners.
Comprehensive syllabi, including lesson plans for approved training.
Procedures for the conduct of examinations and manoeuvre tolerances.
Procedures to require that flight crew members are properly trained and examined on abnormal and emergency conditions.
Procedures for remedial training and subsequent examination of flight crew unable to achieve or maintain required standards.
A process to obtain authority’s approval for subsequent changes to the training manual.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator’s flight crew members, cabin crew members and flight dispatch/flight operations officers have the required training and qualifications before being scheduled to perform flight duties?
criteria: Review surveillance policy and programme to verify that compliance with training requirements is monitored.
Sample completed inspection records/checklists.
During the industry visit, confirm effective implementation by verifying air operators’ training files.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that an air operator establishes a flight dispatch/flight operations officers’ training programme prior to the issuance of an AOC?
pqs.lead: Sample some air operators’ manuals to confirm implementation and compliance with the following:
criteria: Civil air law and regulations.
Aviation instruction.
Use of operations manual.
Aircraft performance.
Navigation.
Flight planning and monitoring.
Rules of the air, communication and air traffic management.
Meteorology.
Mass and balance control.
Use of MEL/configuration deviation list (CDL).
Transport of dangerous goods by air.
Security procedures.
Emergency response plan.
Flight observation.
Recurrent training programme.
Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.
Review evidence during industry visit to confirm implementation.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator maintains, on a recurrent basis, the knowledge, skills and qualifications of flight dispatch/flight operations officers ground instructors?
criteria: Verify that the surveillance programme includes surveillance of flight dispatch instructors.
Review related operations inspectors’ procedures.
Sample flight dispatch/flight operations officers ground instructors training programme and records during the visit to the industry to confirm implementation.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that an air operator establishes a cabin crew training programme prior to the issuance of an AOC?
pqs.lead: Sample some air operators’ manuals to confirm implementation and compliance with the following:
criteria: Basic instructions on the different functions, duties and responsibilities of cabin crew members.
Introduction to aircraft systems and limitations.
Aircraft emergency evacuation, life safety equipment and related information to passengers.
Cabin crew members assignment, coordination and two-way communication.
Knowledge and skills related to the transport of dangerous goods.
Security procedures.
Recurrent training programme including an examination to determine competence.
Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.
Review evidence during industry visit to confirm implementation.

pqs.question: Has the State established a policy and related procedures to ensure that air operators select and appoint cabin crew instructors who meet at least the appropriate minimum experience and knowledge requirements?
criteria: Verify regulations or policy and/or procedure regarding the selection criteria for cabin crew who are charged with tasks as instructors (ground or in flight).

pqs.question: Has the flight operations inspection organization established a surveillance programme to ensure that the appointed instructors for cabin crew maintain their competency?
criteria: Check if surveillance programme includes surveillance of cabin crew instructors.
Review related operations inspectors’ procedures.
Review surveillance policy and programme/plan to confirm maintenance of competency is addressed.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator is managing flight and cabin crew fatigue within the prescriptive limits established by the State?
pqs.lead: Review surveillance policy and programme of air operators to verify that:
criteria: Fatigue is being managed with scheduling practices that allow adequate sleep recovery, limit periods of wakefulness, accommodate circadian influences on sleep and performance, and recognize the impact of workload on fatigued individuals.
Compliance with prescriptive limitation regulations is monitored.
Fatigue is recognized as a safety hazard to be managed using existing SMS processes.
Sample completed inspection records/checklists for flight and cabin crews fatigue management.
During the industry visit, confirm effective implementation by verifying air operators’ fatigue management practices.

pqs.question: If State approves variations to the prescriptive fatigue management regulations, does it do so according to an established process?
criteria: Verify implementation of the operations inspectors’ procedures to issue a variation based on an assessment by the regulator of the comprehensive risk assessment provided by the operator to support their application for a variation, and an application that is within the definition of the 'exceptional circumstances' determined by the regulator.
Confirm effective implementation by verifying records of variation approval.
Verify inclusion of the risk assessment results in the records.
During the industry visit, confirm effective implementation by verifying that the air operator’s variation is in accordance with the approval issued by the operations organization.

pqs.question: If the State approves the use of a Fatigue Risk Management System (FRMS) allowing an operator to move outside of prescribed limits, has the State promulgated FRMS regulations?
criteria: Verify that FRMS regulations have been promulgated for pilots and for cabin crews.
Verify that the content of the FRMS regulations complies with the ICAO requirements.

pqs.question: If the State authorizes air operators to use Fatigue Risk Management System (FRMS), has the flight operations inspection organization established an approval process?
criteria: Verify that there is a documented FRMS approval process.
Verify that the authority requires the operator to identify maximum values for flight times and/or flight duty periods and duty periods as well as minimum values for rest periods.
Verify that the approval process includes the need to define a trial period.
Verify that the requirements for obtaining approval are available to operators.

pqs.question: If the State authorizes air operators to use the Fatigue Risk Management System (FRMS), does the flight operations inspection organization follow the established FRMS approval process?
criteria: Verify that the inspectors reviewed and monitored the operator’s progress during the preparation and trial of the FRMS, as part of the FRMS approval process.

Verify that approval was granted based on it being applied only in those particular operations, routes or trips defined, and evidence that the FRMS delivered a level of safety acceptable to the regulator.

Verify that the authority allows operators to move outside of prescriptive limitations only under the following conditions: a) in accordance with approved variations; b) during a defined trial period of an FRMS as part of FRMS approval process; and c) in accordance with an approved FRMS.
Confirm effective implementation of the approval process by reviewing the FRMS approval documentation of at least one authorized air operator.

pqs.question: If the State authorizes air operators to use Fatigue Risk Management System (FRMS), does the flight operations inspection organization implement an effective FRMS surveillance programme?
criteria: Verify inspectors’ procedures and checklists for FRMS audit/inspection.
Check that records are maintained by the air operators and reviewed by the authority.
Review surveillance programme to ensure that air operators are visited at least once a year.
Verify that the State monitors the FRMS performance indicators agreed upon with the air operator.
Sample completed inspection records/checklists to ensure that all aspects are checked.

pqs.question: If the State authorizes air operators to use Fatigue Risk Management System (FRMS), has the flight operations inspection organization established and implemented enforcement procedures in case deficiencies are identified in an FRMS?
criteria: Verify that there are established processes allowing for different levels of actions to be taken by the authority, from warnings to withdrawal of FRMS approval.
Verify implementation by sampling completed inspection records to ensure that appropriate actions were taken in case deficiencies are identified.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator has established an organizational and management system for the operational control of all flights in accordance with specific operating regulations applicable to aircraft operations with the related policies, processes, standards and procedures?
criteria: Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.

Sample some air operators’ manuals to confirm implementation and compliance with the establishment of operational control system including aircraft tracking and location of an aeroplane in distress, and risk management (assessment and mitigations) when intending to operate over or near conflict zones.
Verify definition of responsibilities, authorities, policies, processes, standards and procedures.

Verify that for the initiation, continuation, diversion, and termination of flights, the operations manual contains the functions and responsibilities of flight crew and flight operations officers/flight dispatchers.
Verify that the authority approves the method of control and supervision of flight operations when it is used.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator has established policy and procedures for flight crew to record and report on: a) routine meteorological observation during: i) en-route; and ii) climb-out phases of the flight; and b) special and other non-routine observations during any phase of the flight; and c) volcanic activity?
criteria: Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.
Sample some air operators’ manuals to confirm implementation and compliance.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator makes available to flight crew and operational personnel, at any aerodrome authorized in its AOC and corresponding operations specifications, pre-flight aeronautical information essential for the safety, regularity and efficiency of air navigation?
pqs.lead: Sample some air operators’ manuals to confirm implementation and compliance with the following:
criteria: Provision of pre-flight information.
That information provided includes elements of the integrated Aeronautical Information Package, maps and charts, NOTAMs, AIP and AIC.
Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.
Review evidence during industry visit to confirm implementation.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization approve the method for determining aerodrome operating minima for aeroplane operators?
criteria: Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.
Sample some air operators’ manuals to confirm implementation and compliance.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization approve the method for determining heliport operating minima for helicopter operators?
criteria: Verify that applicable operations inspectors’ guidance material, manuals, checklists, etc. have been established and implemented.
Sample some air operators’ manuals to confirm implementation and compliance.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator has established an organizational structure which includes the responsibilities and authority for the management of all ground handling functions prior to the issuance of an AOC?
pqs.lead: Verify implementation by checking operations manuals for ground handling structure and lines of responsibilities associated with the following, when applicable:
criteria: Ramp operations.
Passenger services.
Baggage services.
Cabin services.
Weight and balance control.
Ground support equipment.
Fuel services.

Verify implementation of operations inspectors’ procedures to confirm air operator’s establishment of an organizational structure for ground handling functions, with description of related responsibilities and authorities.

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator has established aircraft ground handling training requirements, subcontracting policies, handling processes, procedures and practices for all ground handling operations?
criteria: Verify implementation of the operations inspectors’ procedures as part of the certification process to ensure that air operator’s procedures include training requirements, subcontracting policies, handling processes, and procedures and practices for all ground handling operations.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation (e.g. operations manual, weight and balance manual, handling manual and training programme for ground handlers).

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator maintains permanently its ground handling responsibility, when all or part of the functions and tasks related to ground handling services have been contracted to a service provider?
criteria: Verify the surveillance policy and programme.
Verify that operations inspectors’ procedures and checklists are used for inspection of ground handling responsibilities.
Review completed operations inspectors’ checklists to confirm effective implementation of surveillance programme.

pqs.question: Has the State promulgated regulation on the transport of dangerous goods by air to transpose the provisions of Annex 18?
criteria: Confirm the title, date of promulgation and last amendment of all regulations related to dangerous goods.
Verify that the content of the regulations is consistent, sufficient and properly organized.
Confirm the transposition of the dangerous goods-related provisions of Annex 18.
Verify if the State has incorporated the provisions of Annex 18 and the Technical Instructions (TI or Doc 9284) either directly or by reference.

pqs.question: Has the State notified ICAO of variations from the Technical Instructions (TI or Doc 9284)?
pqs.note: If the State’s policy is to be in full compliance with the TI, it is Satisfactory.
criteria: Verify the process in place for such notification.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented procedures to amend its enabling regulations in the area of dangerous goods subsequent to an Annex 18 amendment?
pqs.note: The establishment of amendment procedures is addressed in LEG PQ 1.009.
criteria: Review documented evidence of the effective implementation of procedures for amending dangerous goods regulations.
Verify that regulations are amended in a timely manner whenever amendments to the related Annex or Technical Instructions (TI or Doc 9284) are received.
Verify the action taken by the State after receipt of the last amendments to Annex 18.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented procedures for identifying and notifying to ICAO differences between ICAO Standards and national regulations in the area of dangerous goods, if any?
pqs.note: The establishment of these procedures is addressed in LEG PQ 1.025. States may also notify differences to ICAO using other means.
criteria: Review documented evidence of the effective implementation.

Confirm that the differences between SARPs and the State legislation and practices are identified and notified to ICAO using the Compliance Checklist/Electronic Filing of Differences (CC/EFOD) system.
Confirm that significant differences between SARPs/PANS/SUPPS and the State regulations and practices are identified and published in the AIP.

pqs.question: Has the State designated and specified to ICAO the appropriate national authority responsible for the transport of dangerous goods by air?
pqs.note: Required even if State does not issue approvals for the transport of dangerous goods. If multiple authorities have responsibilities, identify them individually.
criteria: Cross-check State Aviation Activity Questionnaire (SAAQ) and confirm with ICAO.

pqs.question: Are all the functions and responsibilities of the entities/individuals responsible for dangerous goods clearly defined?
pqs.lead: Review documents detailing functions and responsibilities for inclusion of the following tasks:
criteria: Development and/or revision of specific regulations.
Issuance of exemptions and approvals related to dangerous goods.
Issuance of specific approvals to air operators to transport dangerous goods by air.
Review of dangerous goods training programmes of all air operators.
Review of dangerous goods procedures for all air operators.
Review of dangerous goods training programmes for all entities on the ground involved in the transport of dangerous goods by air.
Inspection of air operators related to dangerous goods.
Inspections of shippers and handling companies related to dangerous goods.
Gathering dangerous goods incidents and accidents information.
Certification of general aviation operations.

pqs.question: Has the State established procedures to: a) approve the dangerous goods training programme of AOC holders; and b) grant specific approval to transport dangerous goods by air?
pqs.lead: Review the dangerous goods inspectors’ procedures and ensure that they include:
criteria: Review of AOC holder’s dangerous goods procedures.
Training programmes for all AOC holders.
On-site inspections before granting of specific approvals to applicants.

pqs.question: Does the State grant specific approvals for the transport of dangerous goods by air?
criteria: Review procedures and process for granting specific approvals.
Review records of specific approvals for transport of dangerous goods.

pqs.question: Does the organization/individual responsible for the transport of dangerous goods by air ensure that: a) authorized air operators have developed and use an acceptance checklist to prevent acceptance of dangerous goods for transport by air unless they are accompanied by a completed dangerous goods transport document; and b) their marking, package, overpack or freight container have been inspected in accordance with the acceptance procedure contained in the Technical Instructions (TI or Doc 9284)?
criteria: Review inspectors’ procedures for issuance of specific approvals to transport dangerous goods.
Sample completed checklists used by authorized air operators.

pqs.question: Does the organization/individual responsible for the transport of dangerous goods by air ensure that authorized air operators will not load dangerous goods into the aircraft unless the appropriate loading, segregation and inspection for damage or leakage procedures are followed?
criteria: Review authorization process and inspection procedures for issuance of specific approvals to transport dangerous goods.
Sample completed checklists or other mechanisms used by authorized air operators.

pqs.question: Does the organization/individual responsible for the transport of dangerous goods by air ensure that: a) the air operator of an aircraft in which dangerous goods are carried provides the pilot-in-command with written information (e.g. Notification to Captain or NOTOC); and b) the written information is retained on the ground and readily accessible to the aerodromes of last departure and next scheduled arrival, as specified in the Technical Instructions (TI or Doc 9284)?
criteria: Review authorization process and inspection procedures for issuance of specific approvals to transport dangerous goods by air.
Sample completed checklists or other documented mechanisms used by authorized air operators.

pqs.question: Does the organization/individual responsible for the transport of dangerous goods by air ensure that the air operator authorized to transport dangerous goods has established in-flight procedures for emergency response for aircraft incidents involving dangerous goods?
criteria: Review authorization process and inspector’s procedures.
Verify implementation of inspector’s procedures.
Sample documented procedures in operations manual.
Review air operator’s procedures during industry visit.

pqs.question: Has the State established procedures for the surveillance of all the air operators related to the transport of dangerous goods by air?
criteria: Verify that there is an inspection policy, which covers authorized or non-authorized air operators, national and foreign operators, with regular and random inspections.
Verify the inspection checklists and guidance used by the inspectors.
Verify that the inspections cover handling responsibilities if subcontracted to an external handling company and that the air operator provides subcontractor with appropriate documents and manuals.

pqs.question: Does the State ensure that passengers are warned as to the types of dangerous goods that they are prohibited or restricted from transporting aboard an aircraft?
pqs.note: This PQ is applicable to all air operators.
criteria: Check the passenger awareness system.

pqs.question: Does the organization/individual responsible for the transport of dangerous goods by air carry out regular and random inspections of all entities, except air operators, involved in the transport of dangerous goods by air to confirm compliance with Annex 18 and the Technical Instructions (TI or Doc 9284)?
pqs.note: The establishment of this PQ is covered in PQ 4.360.
criteria: Sample completed dangerous goods inspection records.
Review if the programme implemented enables the surveillance of all entities.

pqs.question: Has the State established procedures related to the oversight of entities, other than air operators, established in the State and involved in the transport of dangerous goods by air to confirm compliance with dangerous goods regulations?
criteria: Verify that dangerous goods inspection procedures include a review of training programmes as well as regular and random onsite inspections of all entities established in the State as being involved in the transport of dangerous goods by air (shippers and cargo handling companies in particular)[cite: 3].
Review the inspection checklists for each kind of inspection[cite: 3].
Review the inspection policy and the frequency chosen for each type of inspection[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the organization/individual responsible for the transport of dangerous goods by air ensure that the organizations or agencies, which are involved in the transport of dangerous goods by air, have established and implemented initial and recurrent dangerous goods training programmes?
pqs.lead: Verify that the dangerous goods inspectors have reviewed/approved the specific training programmes to be established and maintained by:
criteria: Shippers of dangerous goods, including packers and shippers' agents[cite: 3].
Agencies which perform, on behalf of the air operator, the act of accepting, handling, loading, unloading, transferring or other processing of cargo[cite: 3].
Agencies located at an aerodrome which perform, on behalf of the air operator, the act of processing passengers[cite: 3].
Agencies not located at an aerodrome which perform, on behalf of the air operator, the act of checking in passengers[cite: 3].
Agencies other than air operators involved in processing cargo[cite: 3].
Designated Postal Operators (has to be approved by State)[cite: 3].
Agencies engaged in the security screening of passengers and their baggage[cite: 3].
Any other person or agency performing a function on behalf of the air operator[cite: 3].
Review the files for each type of agencies[cite: 3].
Sample dangerous goods training records to confirm implementation[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Are dangerous goods training programmes of air operators reviewed and approved by the organization/individual responsible for the transport of dangerous goods by air before specific approval is granted?
pqs.note: This PQ is for air operators applying for a specific approval to transport dangerous goods.
criteria: Review certification records[cite: 3].
Verify air operator’s training programme to ensure that it covers all employees[cite: 3].
Review and sample air operator’s training records to confirm implementation[cite: 3].

pqs.question: For air operators which choose not to transport dangerous goods, are the dangerous goods training programmes intended to help the ground and flight personnel recognize and refuse dangerous goods, reviewed and approved by the organization/individual responsible for the transport of dangerous goods by air?
pqs.note: This PQ is for air operators that do not intend to transport dangerous goods.
criteria: Verify implementation of related procedure for the review and approval of training programmes[cite: 3].
Sample operations manuals of AOC holders not authorized to transport dangerous goods by air[cite: 3].
Sample dangerous goods recognition training programmes and records during the industry visit[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Are dangerous goods procedures of air operators reviewed by the organization/individual responsible for the transport of dangerous goods by air, as part of the flight safety documents system of the air operator?
pqs.note: This PQ is for air operators that do not intend to transport dangerous goods.
pqs.lead: Verify implementation of dangerous goods inspectors’ procedures to ensure:
criteria: Exceptions related to passengers are covered[cite: 3].

That air operators not authorized to transport dangerous goods have a policy to not transport spare parts for maintenance purposes that should be categorized as dangerous goods (Company Material [COMAT])[cite: 3].
Procedures to report incident involving undeclared dangerous goods are covered[cite: 3].
Review certification records[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization or the organization/individual responsible for the transport of dangerous goods by air ensure that dangerous goods procedures and training programmes are incorporated in either the operations manual or a separate document as part of the flight safety documents system?
pqs.note: This PQ is for air operators applying for a specific approval to transport dangerous goods.
criteria: Verify regulations[cite: 3].
Review certification process and inspectors’ procedures[cite: 3].
Sample operations manual or separate document as part of flight safety documents system[cite: 3].
Sample training programmes and records during the industry visit[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the organization/individual responsible for the transport of dangerous goods by air ensure that the air operator has established procedures to convey information to emergency services and to appropriate authorities in the event of an incident or accident of an aircraft carrying dangerous goods?
pqs.note: This PQ is for air operators applying for a specific approval to transport dangerous goods.
criteria: Review authorization process and inspector’s procedures[cite: 3].
Sample documented procedures in operations manual[cite: 3].
Confirm knowledge of these procedures during industry visit[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Has the organization/individual responsible of the transport of dangerous goods by air established and implemented: a) procedures to take appropriate actions in case of violation (e.g. enforcement and penalties), and b) a system to track identified deficiencies and to ensure timely resolution?
pqs.note: This PQ is applicable to all entities, including air operators.
criteria: Review the system to inform the entities inspected of the deficiencies detected, how their corrective action plan is assessed, etc[cite: 3].
Sample cases of discrepancies or violations and the actions taken by the State[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Has the State established and implemented a process for reporting dangerous goods incidents and accidents, with procedures for investigating and compiling information concerning accidents or incidents involving dangerous goods, which occur in its territory and which involve the transport of dangerous goods originating in or destined for another State?
criteria: Review the incident reporting system (this includes undeclared, misdeclared and unintentional release of dangerous goods) in place for investigation and for compiling information[cite: 3].
See definition of dangerous goods incident[cite: 3].
Review promotion done by the State to ensure that such incidents are reported[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Has the State established procedures for the acceptance or approval of aircraft leasing arrangements?
pqs.lead: Verify procedures established for the type of leases allowed (dry, wet, or damp leases) address as a minimum flight crew training, cabin crew training, operational control, dispatch and flight watch, and crew members scheduling, and also the following approvals as applicable:
criteria: The method for establishing minimum flight altitudes (4.2.7.3)[cite: 3].
The method of determining aerodrome operating minima (4.2.8.1)[cite: 3].
Flight time, flight duty periods and rest periods (4.10.2a)[cite: 3].
EDTO (4.7)[cite: 3].
Aircraft-specific minimum equipment list (MEL) (6.1.3)[cite: 3].
Performance-based navigation operations (7.2.2 b)[cite: 3].
RVSM operations (7.2.6 b))[cite: 3].
Procedures for electronic navigation data management (7.5.1)[cite: 3].
Training in the transport of dangerous goods (9.3.1)[cite: 3].
Pilot-in-command area, route and aerodrome qualifications (9.4.3.5)[cite: 3].
Use of flight simulation training devices[cite: 3].
Verify procedures for coordination with airworthiness and licensing for related requirements[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Has the State implemented a process for the acceptance or approval of aircraft leasing arrangements, including coordination on airworthiness-related aspects?
pqs.lead: Verify implementation of the procedures to address as a minimum flight crew training, cabin crew training, operational control, dispatch and flight watch, and crew members scheduling, and also the following as applicable:
criteria: The method for establishing minimum flight altitudes (4.2.7.3)[cite: 3].
The method of determining aerodrome operating minima (4.2.8.1)[cite: 3].
Flight time, flight duty periods and rest periods (4.10.2a)[cite: 3].
EDTO (4.7)[cite: 3].
Aircraft-specific minimum equipment list (MEL) (6.1.3)[cite: 3].
Performance-based navigation operations (7.2.2 b)[cite: 3].
RVSM operations (7.2.6 b))[cite: 3].
Procedures for electronic navigation data management (7.5.1)[cite: 3].
Training in the transport of dangerous goods (9.3.1)[cite: 3].
Pilot-in-command area, route and aerodrome qualifications (9.4.3.5)[cite: 3].
Use of flight simulation training devices[cite: 3].
Verify coordination on airworthiness-related aspects[cite: 3].

pqs.question: If the State has entered into any agreement under the provisions of Article 83 bis to the Chicago Convention, has the State established procedures for the transfer of licensing and operations-related tasks and functions, including notification to ICAO?
pqs.note: Verify the process to transfer out, not in.
criteria: Verify procedures for evaluating capability of State accepting oversight duties[cite: 3].
Verify guidance on the recognition of licences and certificates[cite: 3].
Verify that notification to ICAO of transfer agreements is included in the procedure[cite: 3].
Verify in the agreement or in another document which of the two parties has the responsibility for the notification to ICAO[cite: 3].

pqs.question: If the State has entered into any agreement under the provisions of Article 83 bis to the Chicago Convention as State of Registry, does the State carry out an assessment of the capability of the State of the Operator accepting the transfer?
pqs.note: Verify the process to transfer out, not in.
criteria: Verify procedures for evaluating the capability of the State accepting oversight duties to cover provisions in Annexes 1, 2 and 6[cite: 3].
Review implementation of related procedures[cite: 3].
Sample an agreement, together with its agreement summary, to confirm assessment and notification to ICAO[cite: 3].
Review the method in place to ensure that a certified true copy of the agreement summary is on board the aircraft[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Has the State implemented a process to issue approvals for the transport of dangerous goods by air?
pqs.note: The approval referred to in this PQ is different than dangerous goods specific approval issued to an air operator as a part of operations specifications associated with the AOC.
criteria: Review application forms and their contents[cite: 3].

Review the procedure to assess the application forms and the policy and criteria to issue approvals as State of Origin or State of Operator and as applicable (deviation stipulated in the Technical Instructions (TI) or Doc 9284)[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Has the State implemented a process to grant exemptions from the provisions of the Technical Instructions for the safe transport of dangerous goods by air, when the State is the State of Origin, Operator, Transit, Overflight and Destination?
pqs.note: The legal basis for the granting of exemptions is addressed in LEG PQ 1.027. The establishment of a policy and associated procedures for the granting of exemptions is addressed in LEG PQ 1.028.
criteria: Review completed application forms and their contents[cite: 3].
Review the procedure to assess the application forms and the policy and criteria to grant exemptions (e.g. extreme urgency, other forms of transport inappropriate, public interest, etc.)[cite: 3].
Ensure that the procedure includes determination of equivalent level of safety[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator has established: a) policy and procedures to enable cabin crew to discreetly communicate to flight crew in the event of suspicious activity or security breaches in the passenger cabin; and b) policy and procedures with respect to flight crew compartment access?
criteria: Verify State regulations[cite: 3].
Review certification process[cite: 3].
Review operations inspectors’ procedures[cite: 3].
Sample operations manual or security manual to confirm implementation[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator has, on board all its aircraft, a checklist of the procedures to be followed: a) in searching for a bomb; b) for inspecting an aircraft for concealed weapons, explosives and other dangerous devices; and c) appropriate course of action to be taken in case a bomb or suspicious object is found?
criteria: Verify certification process and operations inspectors’ procedures[cite: 3].
Sample operations manual or security manual to confirm implementation of such guidance[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the State ensure that a security training programme for flight and cabin crew is established by the operator and approved by the State before the AOC is granted?
pqs.lead: Review the certification process and procedures to ensure that the programme is reviewed in detail and sample training programme to confirm that the following are addressed:
criteria: Security of the flight crew compartment[cite: 3].
Aircraft search procedure checklist[cite: 3].
Determination of the seriousness of any occurrences[cite: 3].
Crew communication and coordination[cite: 3].
Appropriate self-defence responses[cite: 3].
Use, as authorized by the State of the Operator, of non-lethal protective devices assigned to crew members[cite: 3].
Understanding of behaviour of terrorists[cite: 3].
Live situational training exercises regarding various threat conditions[cite: 3].
Post-flight concerns for the crew[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Has the State promulgated regulations and additional requirements for an air operator applying for approval to operate a single-engine turbine powered aeroplane at night and/or in Instrument Meteorological Conditions (IMC)?
pqs.note: If State’s requirements do not allow such operations, the PQ is “Not Applicable”.
criteria: Verify regulations[cite: 3].
Review additional requirements, such as engine trend monitoring system, redundant electrical generating systems, radio altimeter, airborne weather radar, etc[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Do the AOC and associated operations specifications issued by the authority contain all the elements and follow the format/layout outlined in the relevant provisions of Annex 6?
pqs.lead: Verify certification process and operations inspectors’ procedures, and sample AOCs and operations specifications to verify if they contain the following:
criteria: State of the Operator and issuing authority[cite: 3].
AOC number and expiration date[cite: 3].
Operator name, trading name (if different) & address[cite: 3].
Date of issue and name, signature and title of authority representative[cite: 3].
The location, in a controlled document carried on board where the contact details of operational management can be found[cite: 3].
Verify that the AOC and operations specifications follow the layout of A6, Part I, App. 6, para. 3 or A6, Part III, App. 3, para. 3 to the Chicago Convention[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Has the flight operations inspection organization established procedures for the issuance of other approvals and authorizations contained in the operations specifications?
pqs.lead: Review certification process and operations procedures for issuance of specific approvals (RVSM, EDTO including conducting applicants’ safety risk assessment, PBN authorization required (AR), and other), and verify that coordination with airworthiness includes:
criteria: Documented process, including inspectors’ procedures, inspectors’ guidance, and inspectors’ checklists[cite: 3].
Defined areas of responsibility[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator has taken into account, reviewed and implemented all regulations before operations specifications are granted?
criteria: Verify implementation of the certification process and operations inspectors’ procedures[cite: 3].
Review the related air operators procedures for: a) RVSM; b) EDTO; c) PBN authorization required (AR); and d) other[cite: 3].
Review implementation of documented coordination procedures with airworthiness[cite: 3].
Sample issued AOCs/operations specifications[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Has the State established approval procedures for the conduct of CAT II and III instrument approaches?
criteria: Verify procedures for approval of such operations[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization carry out an operations evaluation for the conduct of CAT II and III instrument approaches?
criteria: Verify the process used, including flight crew training[cite: 3].
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation[cite: 3].
Confirm that CAT II and III instrument approach and landing operations shall not be authorized unless runway visual range (RVR) information is provided[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Has the flight operations inspection organization established a formal inspection policy and procedures to be used by operations inspectors for conducting inspections of AOC holders and foreign air operators?
criteria: Identification of inspections to be carried out for AOC holders and foreign air operators[cite: 3].
Guidelines for scheduling inspections and intervals between each inspection[cite: 3].
Standardized checklists for inspections[cite: 3].
Inspection/audit process[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Is a formal surveillance programme implemented to verify that all AOC holders in the State comply, on a continuing basis, with national regulations, international standards, AOCs and corresponding operations specifications?
pqs.note: Inspections include scheduled, unscheduled and unannounced inspections.
criteria: Verify surveillance programme for previous and current year (planned and completed)[cite: 3].
Confirm appropriate frequency of inspection (may be based on proven safety indicators or results of previous inspections)[cite: 3].
Sample inspection records of surveillance of air operators[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the established surveillance programme monitor the AOC holders’ financial condition and any negative trends?
criteria: Verify surveillance programme[cite: 3].
Verify monitoring procedures[cite: 3].
Sample inspections records[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the surveillance policy and programme of the CAA include the necessary processes and procedures for coordination among its different departments or directorates (i.e. transportation, finances, legal, operations, personnel licensing, airworthiness, etc.)?
criteria: Verify that surveillance policy provides for coordination[cite: 3].
Review operations inspectors’ procedures[cite: 3].
Verify surveillance programme records to confirm that all relevant aspects from other departments are reviewed (like ramp inspections)[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Is a formal surveillance programme implemented to verify that foreign air operators comply, on a continuing basis, with international standards, AOCs and corresponding operations specifications?
pqs.note: Inspections include scheduled, unscheduled and unannounced inspections.
criteria: Verify surveillance programme for previous and current year (planned and completed)[cite: 3].
Confirm appropriate frequency of inspection (may be based on proven safety indicators or results of previous inspections)[cite: 3].
Sample inspection records of surveillance of foreign air operators[cite: 3].

pqs.question: What actions are taken by the CAA if the deficiencies identified during the conduct of inspections are not rectified in a timely manner?
pqs.lead: Verify the system for AOC holders of the State and foreign air operators, and:
criteria: Verify operations inspectors’ procedures and enforcement procedures[cite: 3].
Verify examples of graduated actions taken, such as: a) imposition of fines; b) restrictions/limitations; and c) suspension/revocation of certificate[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the surveillance programme include ramp inspections of aircraft operated by AOC holders and foreign air operators, based on risk?
pqs.note: Inspections include scheduled, unscheduled and unannounced inspections.
pqs.lead: Review evidence on policy on items to be checked during ramp inspections, taking into account both high-risk items and all other items over a series of inspections, such as:
criteria: Flight deck – general (e.g. condition, emergency exit, equipment)[cite: 3].
Flight deck — documentation (e.g. manuals, checklists, route guide, MEL, documents to be carried on board & flight deck safety equipment)[cite: 3].
Cabin/Safety[cite: 3].
Aircraft external condition, e.g. previous structural repairs; obvious damage; leakage (e.g. apparent corrosion)[cite: 3].
Cargo (condition of cargo compartment and containers; dangerous goods; safety of cargo on board)[cite: 3].
General (additional remarks; refuelling; language for communication)[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Has the flight operations inspection organization implemented a documented process or a system to track the deficiencies identified and to accept/validate the corrective actions taken by the air operators?
criteria: Sample inspection reports and correspondence with the air operator[cite: 3].
Verify the system to track the deficiencies within the CAA[cite: 3].
Confirm that deficiencies are rectified in a timely manner[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization maintain a system which monitors and records progress, including actions taken by the air operator in resolving identified safety issues, to make it possible to track past deficiencies and regulatory non-compliance?
criteria: Review operations inspectors’ procedures[cite: 3].
Review air operator’s files[cite: 3].
Verify monitoring system to ensure that the CAA takes into account past deficiencies when establishing its surveillance programme and performing risk assessment[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator has established a procedure for the crew to evaluate a traveller with a suspected communicable disease, based on the presence of a fever and certain other signs or symptoms?
criteria: Review operations inspectors’ procedures to ensure that they check air operators’ related procedures[cite: 3].
Confirm effective implementation by verifying that an air operator’s operations manual or cabin crew manual contains related procedures[cite: 3].
Check that the procedure includes the transmission, as required, of a General Declaration form to the State authorities[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the flight operations inspection organization ensure that the air operator has established procedures for the pilot-in-command to report promptly to air traffic control (ATC) a suspected communicable disease, with transmission of the following information: a) aircraft identification; b) departure aerodrome; c) destination aerodrome; d) estimated time of arrival; e) number of persons on board; f) number of suspected case(s) on board; and g) nature of the public health risk, if known?
pqs.lead: Review operations inspectors’ procedures to ensure that they check air operators’ related procedures, and confirm effective implementation by verifying that an air operator’s operations manual has procedures for reporting suspected communicable diseases, including transmission of the following information:
criteria: Aircraft identification[cite: 3].
Departure aerodrome[cite: 3].
Destination aerodrome[cite: 3].
Estimated time of arrival[cite: 3].
Number of persons on board[cite: 3].
Number of suspected case(s) on board[cite: 3].
Nature of the public health risk, if known[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Has the State promulgated specific operating regulations to require operators of aeroplanes or helicopters authorized to conduct international commercial air transport (air operators) to implement a safety management system (SMS)?
pqs.note: This PQ is only applicable to commercial air transport operators in accordance with Annex 6 Parts I and III.
criteria: Require air operators to implement an SMS that is acceptable to the State[cite: 3].
Require the SMS to be established in accordance with the SMS framework elements contained in Appendix 2 to Annex 19[cite: 3].
Comply with the latest amendments to Annex 19[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the State ensure that the personnel responsible for the acceptance and continuous surveillance of air operators SMS are qualified to perform their functions and responsibilities?
pqs.note: The framework components and elements in Appendix 2 to Annex 19 are listed in PQ 4.901.
criteria: The State has identified competency requirements for personnel responsible for the acceptance and continuous surveillance of an SMS[cite: 3].
The content of the training programme addresses the SMS framework elements contained in Appendix 2 to Annex 19[cite: 3].

The training also includes (but is not limited to) the following: a) evaluation and acceptance of an air operator’ SMS; b) evaluation and acceptance of an air operator’s safety performance indicators (SPIs); c) continuous surveillance of the air operator’s SMS in a safety management environment; d) hazard identification and risk management processes and methodologies; e) protection of safety data, safety information and related sources; f) enforcement under the framework of SMS; and g) soft skills (e.g. effective communication skills, negotiation skills, conflict resolution, etc.)[cite: 3].
A training plan has been implemented[cite: 3].
Initial, OJT and recurrent training, as applicable, with specified-time periods are included[cite: 3].
Training records are systematically retained[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Has the State provided guidance material to the industry on the establishment and management of an SMS?
pqs.note: The framework components and elements in Appendix 2 to Annex 19 are listed in PQ 4.901.
criteria: Includes the processes for the establishment and management of an SMS[cite: 3].
Addresses the SMS framework elements contained in Appendix 2 to Annex 19[cite: 3].
Is updated and made accessible to air operators[cite: 3].
Corresponds with activities conducted by the air operators[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Has the State established procedures for the acceptance of air operators SMS?
pqs.note: The framework components and elements in Appendix 2 to Annex 19, are listed in PQ 4.901.
criteria: Describe the processes for the acceptance of an SMS, where the SMS manual at least includes: a) safety policy and safety objectives; b) reference to any applicable regulatory SMS requirements; c) system description; d) safety accountabilities and key safety personnel; e) voluntary and mandatory safety reporting system processes and procedures; f) hazard identification and safety risk assessment processes and procedures; g) safety investigation procedures; h) procedures for establishing and monitoring safety performance indicators; i) SMS training processes and procedures and communication; j) safety communication processes and procedures; k) internal audit procedures; l) management of change procedures; m) SMS documentation management procedures; and n) where applicable, coordination of emergency response planning[cite: 3].
Address the SMS framework elements contained in Appendix 2 to Annex 19[cite: 3].
Are updated and made accessible to personnel responsible for the acceptance of an SMS[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Has the State established procedures for the continuous surveillance of air operators SMS?
criteria: Describe the processes for the continuous surveillance of an SMS[cite: 3].
Are updated and made accessible to personnel responsible for the surveillance of an SMS[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Has the State accepted the SMS of individual air operators?
criteria: Review evidence of implemented procedures for accepting an SMS including the maintenance of SMS operational records[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the State ensure that the air operators identify hazards, assess identified safety risks, and mitigate them appropriately?
pqs.note: This verification needs to be reviewed during the industry visit.
pqs.lead: Verify that the State ensures the air operators maintain a process to:
criteria: Identify hazards associated with their aviation products or services[cite: 3].
Assess safety risks[cite: 3].
Mitigate safety risks[cite: 3].
Actively identify hazards, assess safety risks and mitigate them appropriately[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the State ensure that safety performance indicators and targets established by air operators are acceptable to the State?
pqs.note: SPIs acceptance may also be done later in the implementation process. This verification needs to be reviewed during the industry visit.
criteria: Review the State’s mechanism for accepting individual air operators’ safety performance indicators (SPIs) and targets (when applicable) to verify that proposed SPIs and respective targets (as applicable), are developed in a manner that supports the effort to meet their safety objectives, and are formulated in a manner to ensure that they are specific, measurable, achievable, relevant and timebound[cite: 3].
Verify that the State ensures that the air operators’ proposed SPIs are reviewed and accepted as part of the SMS acceptance[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the State ensure the air operators monitor their safety performance?
pqs.lead: Verify that the State has established and implemented a mechanism to ensure that the air operators periodically review its safety performance including the means to:
criteria: Validate the effectiveness of safety risks controls[cite: 3].
Validate the identification of changes that may affect the level of safety risks associated with their aviation products or services[cite: 3].
Validate the identification and management of the safety risks that may arise from those changes[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the State ensure that the air operators personnel involved in SMS are qualified to perform their functions and responsibilities?
pqs.note: The scope of the safety training programme should be appropriate to each individual’s involvement in the SMS. Review the training records of the air operators’ personnel during the industry visit.
criteria: Verify that the State has established and implemented a mechanism to validate that the air operators’ personnel involved in SMS are qualified to perform their functions and responsibilities[cite: 3].
Sample surveillance activities records[cite: 3].

pqs.question: Does the State ensure that the air operators continually promote safety?
pqs.note: This PQ is applicable to commercial air transport operators.
pqs.lead: Verify that the State ensures the air operators develop and maintain a formal mean for safety communication that includes:
criteria: Ensuring personnel are aware of the SMS to a degree commensurate with their positions[cite: 3].
Conveying safety-critical information[cite: 3].
Explaining why particular actions are taken to improve safety[cite: 3].
Explaining why safety procedures are introduced or changed[cite: 3].
Have considered communicating to external organizations any of their safety related information[cite: 3].
